3.why: The answer is built left to right in sorted order.

4.why: This start may shrink as overlaps to its left are absorbed.
4.invariant: new_start is the left edge of the interval being inserted.

5.why: This end may grow as overlaps to its right are absorbed.
5.invariant: new_end is the right edge of the interval being inserted.

6.why: We walk the existing intervals once, in order.
6.invariant: i marks the interval under consideration.

7.happening: There are {len(intervals)} existing intervals to scan.
7.why: The scan stops when i reaches n.
7.invariant: n is fixed for the whole scan.

8.happening: Phase 1: skipping intervals that end before the new interval starts.
8.why: Intervals entirely to the left never overlap the new one.

9.why: It ends before the new interval, so it stays as-is.
9.invariant: result holds the untouched left intervals.

10.why: Phase 1 continues until an interval reaches the new one.

11.happening: Phase 2: absorbing intervals that overlap the new one.
11.why: Any interval starting on or before new_end overlaps it.

12.happening: Pulling the merged start left to the smaller of {new_start} and {intervals[i][0]}.
12.why: The union starts at the earliest of the two starts.

13.happening: Pushing the merged end right to the larger of {new_end} and {intervals[i][1]}.
13.why: The union ends at the latest of the two ends.

14.why: It is now folded into the merged new interval.

15.happening: Placing the merged new interval [{new_start}, {new_end}] into result.
15.why: Every overlap is folded in, so the interval is final.
15.invariant: result now contains the inserted interval.

16.happening: Phase 3: copying the remaining later intervals.
16.why: Everything left sits entirely after the new interval.
16.invariant: These never overlap the new one.

17.why: It starts after the new interval ends.

18.why: Phase 3 copies until the scan ends.

19.happening: Returning {len(result)} intervals with the new one merged in.

init.happening: Reading the new interval and preparing the scan.
init.why: We need its start, end, and a place to build the answer.
init.invariant: result will be sorted and non-overlapping.

loop.happening: Scanning the existing intervals in three phases.
loop.why: Before, overlapping, and after the new interval.
loop.invariant: i sweeps forward through the intervals.

update.happening: Copying an interval or absorbing an overlap.
update.why: Overlaps grow the new interval; others are copied unchanged.
update.invariant: result holds everything processed so far.

move.why: Each interval is visited exactly once.

return.invariant: result is the sorted, non-overlapping answer.
